remaining_gap.reason: Stateless-reset suppression still needs connection-state awareness.
Retransmitting a terminal close packet after additional traffic needs a send path.
Bounding terminal retransmissions needs connection-lifecycle policy.
Attempting stateless reset after close needs receive-path and stateful termination logic.
Invalid Initial discard depends on handshake authentication and packet-processing pipeline.
Safe discard after reverting side effects needs a reversible receive pipeline.
RESET_STREAM instigation for application-level errors needs application-protocol and stream-state orchestration.
Only the application protocol can instigate RESET_STREAM, which is not modeled in the current helper layer.
Stream termination policy is owned by the application protocol, not the current QUIC helper surface.
STOP_SENDING-driven automatic RESET_STREAM requires an application-protocol callback surface.
Premature-cancel handling rules are application-specific and remain outside the current implementation.

notes: Wire-format CONNECTION_CLOSE support is now present for both transport and application closes, and the classifier no longer treats application close type 0x1d as ack-eliciting.
The remaining section 11 clauses are endpoint- and application-lifecycle policies that need connection-state and application-protocol surfaces not present in this repository slice.
